a) The Municipal Treasurer to prepare the RIS to support every issuance of supplies and inventories to requisitioning units or end users; prepare and submit the SSMI to the Accounting Unit as basis for the recording of related expenses; b) The Municipal Accountant, Municipal GSO and Municipal Treasurer shall prepare and maintain complete Supply Ledger Cards and Stock Cards, respectively, conduct periodic reconciliation of their records; and update the Report on the Physical Count of Inventories (RCPI) to the Office of the Auditor not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year; c) The Inventory Committee conduct physical count of inventories at least semi-annually, prepare and submit the Report on the Physical Count of Inventories (RCPI) to the Office of the Auditor.
We recommend that the Municipal GSO shall prepare and submit to the Audit Team the monthly report of fuel consumption and the Report of fuel consumption per vehicle as required.

a) The Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer (LDRRMO) shall: Prepare and submit the required annual LDRRMFIP and to review the accuracy of the appropriation for pre and post disaster programs, projects and activities and particularly for the Quick Response Fund; b) Prepare and submit the Report of Utilization Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund within 15 days after the end of each month, using Annex J-1 format, as provided for under the said COA Circular; and c) Direct the Municipal Budget Officer to maintain RAAO for QRF, MOOEandCO.

We recommend that the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant prepare SCBAA as of December 31,2021 and submit to the Audit Team. We further recommend that for the ensuing years, the SCBAA should be submitted immediately to the Audit Team for review.

1.1. We recommended for the Municipal Accountant to do a restatement of the Financial Statements to correct material errors and to immediately submit to the Office of the Auditor the updated set of Financial Statements.

1.12. Therefore, we recommended the Management to:
(a) Create an Inventory Committee composed of adequate number of
members to be able to complete the physical inventory. The Inventory
Committee shall have at least one member each from the Accounting
and Property Units of the Municipality.
(b) Coordinate with the Property Division/Unit to strategize on how to
conduct and complete the physical inventory within prescribed period.
(c) Prepare a Physical Inventory Plan (PIP) which shall be approved by
the Local Chief Executive and submit a copy the Auditor.
(d) Conduct physical count of all PPE in accordance with the PIP.
(e) Prepare and submit to the Auditor the Report on Physical Count of
PPE (RPCPPE)/Physical Inventory Report.
(f) Direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer acting as
the General Services Officer to prepare and maintain subsidiary
ledgers and property ledger card for each PPE.

2.6. This Office recommended to the head of the agency to instruct the
Accountant to prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statement (BRS) on a
monthly basis in accordance with COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October
2, 1996 and maintain the corresponding Subsidiary Ledgers for auditor's
immediate review and verification.

3.5. Therefore, we recommended that the Municipal Treasurer:
(a) To strictly adhere to the rules provided for in the MNGAS Manual for
LGUs.
(b) To maintain and update daily the Cashbook for Cash in Treasury and
Cash in Bank accounts.

4.7. We recommended the Municipal Accountant to do a restatement of the
Financial Statements to correct material errors and to immediately submit
to the Office ofthe Auditor of the updated set of Financial Statements. We
further recommend for the ensuing years, the Municipal Accountant be
circumspect in the preparation of the Financial Statements.

6.13. We recommended the Management to prepare and maintain RAAO

the beginning of the year. Furthermore, it should contain all PPA s to

We recommended the Management to: a. Adhere to the existing laws, rules and regulations in the supply and property management for LGUs. b. Create an Inventory and Disposal Committee who shall be responsible to conduct a complete physical inventory count, annually, as required. c. The General Services Officer or the Municipal Treasurer shall likewise maintain stock cards property plant and equipment to account for the receipt and disposition of the same. The balance per property card should always reconcile with the ledger cards of the accounting unit. d. The Accounting Office should maintain the prescribed PPELC which should be reconciled with the RPCPPE to establish correctness of the PPE accounts appearing in the LGU financial statements.

Recommendations:
a. Adhere to the existing laws, rules and regulations in the supply and
property management for LGUs;
b. Create an Inventory and Disposal Committee who shall be responsible to
conduct a complete physical inventory count, annually, as required;
c. The General Services Officer or the Municipal Treasurer shall likewise
maintain stock cards property plant and equipment to account for the receipt and
disposition of the same. The balance per property card should always reconcile
with the ledger cards of the accounting unit; and
d. The Accounting Office should maintain the prescribed PPELC which should be
reconciled with the RPCPPE to establish correctness of the PPE accounts
appearing in the LGU financial statements.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor should direct the department heads of
the accounting and property offices to:
a. Account for the beginning balances of the CIP accounts by submitting
subsidiary records; and
b. Maintain a Construction in Progress Ledger Card for each project and per
fund cluster. The Accounting staff in charge in maintaining the CIPLC shall
record promptly the construction costs and other information and associated
costs about the asset constructed.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the head of agency shall instruct the Accountant to prepare
the Bank Reconciliation Statement (BRS) on a monthly basis in accordance with
COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996. This is to facilitate the immediate
verification so that the necessary adjusting entries for any discrepancies or errors or
other reconciling items requiring corrections can be made, in order to reflect the
correct balance of cash accounts in the financial statements. Also, bank
reconciliation statements must be made for all bank accounts of the Municipality.

Recommendations:
We recommended Management to strictly comply with the guidelines of the LBC,
likewise full disclosure policy of the government must be adhered. We recommended
further to submit a written explanation on the above audit observation.

In view of the foregoing, we recommend to the Management to:
a. Prepare and Submit the Monthly Report of Fund Utilization and Status of
Implementation of PPAs funded from Bayanihan Grant for CY 2020; and
b. Indicate, specifically, as to the location of three (3) conspicuous public places
where the accumulated reports are posted. And a photo of the posted reports
to substantiate compliance.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Management strictly adhere with the provision of RA
7160, RA 9184 and LBC 125 in the preparation and submission of the supplemental
budget and revised annual procurement plan (APP).

Recommendations:
We recommended that management should comply with Section 3.7 of Local Budget
Circular (LBC) 125, Section 313 of RA 7160 and Section 107 of MNGAS in taking up
the Bayanihan Grant and submit to the Office of the Auditor its subsidiary ledger.

We recommend to the Management to strictly observe the provisions in Section 3.7
of Local Budget Circular (LBC) 125, Section 313 of RA 7160 and Section 107 of
MNGAS and to submit Subsidiary Ledger for the Bayanihan Grant to the Office of
the Audit Team Leader.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Management furnish the Audit Team with the documentary
requirements of implementation and liquidation of all funds transferred by the
National Government Agencies (NGAs) as provided for in item 3.1.2 and Item 3.1.3
of COA Circular No. 2012 to evaluate whether the funds were utilized within the
specified purpose.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensures that the documentary
requirements of all claims are complete before affixing his signature in Box A of the
Disbursement Voucher.

We recommend to the Management to strictly observe Section 4.6 of PD 1445 and
COA Circular No. 2012-001 and ensure that all claims against government funds
shall be supported with complete documentation.

We recommended that the Management furnish the Auditor a copy of the CY2020
GAD Plan and Accomplishment Report for GAD showing the programs, projects and
activities undertaken during the year. We further recommend that for CY 2021, the
GAD Plan and Budget should be submitted immediately to the Audit Team and that
GAD Quarterly Accomplishment Report should also be submitted within the
prescribed period.

We recommend to the Management to submit the Office of the Audit Team Leader
the CY2020 GAD Accomplishment Report and Approved GAD Plan CY2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LGU shall submit the mandatory Quarterly, Semestral and Annual Reports within the prescribed period as stated in COA Circulars pertaining to these reports and to facilitate the gathering and consolidation of information required by the Commission.

We recommended that the Management shall review the current accounting and reporting system of the LGU starting from the determination of an accounting transaction, recording of the transactions, posting the transactions to the journals, registries, subsidiary and general journals, preparation of trial balance to the preparation of financial statements to check and test whether it is in accordance with the established accounting standards, rules and regulations. Immediately correct and rectify all errors and inaccuracies observed.

We recommend that the head of agency shall instruct the Accountant to prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statement (BRS) on a monthly basis in accordance with COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996 for auditor’s immediate review and verification and to facilitate the immediate preparation of the necessary correction/adjusting entries for any discrepancies or errors or other reconciling items requiring corrections in order to reflect the correct balance of cash accounts in the financial statements. Also, bank reconciliation statements must be made for all bank accounts of the Municipality.

a. Review the current accounting and reporting system of the LGU starting from the determination of an accounting transaction, recording of the transactions, posting the transactions to the journals, registries, subsidiary and general journals, preparation of trial balance to the preparation of financial statements to check and test whether it is in accordance with the established accounting standards, rules and regulations. Immediately correct and rectify all errors and inaccuracies observed.

b. Strictly maintain General Ledgers (GLs) for EACH (emphasis given) Balance Sheet and Income Statement Account, as well as Subsidiary Ledgers (SLs) to those accounts/category/accounting responsibility. Ensure also that the account titles/names used and the amount which appears in the GL are the same with that of the SL and the Financial Statements.

c. Make sure also that the beginning balances carried forward in a particular year in the GL and SL should be the same with the ending balances in the previous years.

d. Prepare General Ledger for each account presented in the Financial Statements.

e. Maintain all the necessary journals, registries, ledgers and other relevant supporting documents/ reports required by established accounting standards

Recommendation:
We recommend that the head of agency shall instruct the Accountant to
prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statement (BRS) on a monthly basis in accordance
with COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996 for auditor’s immediate
review and verification and to facilitate the immediate preparation of the necessary
correction/adjusting entries for any discrepancies or errors or other reconciling
items requiring corrections in order to reflect the correct balance of cash accounts
in the financial statements. Also, bank reconciliation statements must be made for
all bank accounts of the Municipality.

Recommendations:
We recommend the Management to:
a. Adhere to the existing laws, rules and regulations in the supply and property
management for LGUs.
b. Create an Inventory and Disposal Committee who shall be responsible to
conduct a complete physical inventory count, annually, as required.
c. The General Services Officer or the Municipal Treasurer shall likewise
maintain stock cards property plant and equipment to account for the receipt
and disposition of the same. The balance per property card should always
reconcile with the ledger cards of the accounting unit.
d. The Accounting Office should maintain the prescribed PPELC which
should be reconciled with the RPCPPE to establish correctness of the
PPE accounts appearing in the LGU financial statements.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor shall instruct the concerned
department heads of the accounting and property to:
a. Account for the beginning balances of the CIP accounts by submitting
subsidiary records.
b. Maintain a Construction in Progress Ledger Card for each project and
per fund cluster. The Accounting staff in charge in maintaining the
CIPLC shall record promptly the construction costs and other
information and associated costs about the asset constructed.

Recommendations:
We reiterate our recommendation for the Management to:
1. Require the Municipal Accountant to submit the Report of Checks Issued and
Report of Collections and Deposits daily to the Accounting Office pursuant to
Section 55 on the Manual of NGAS and to facilitate submission to the Office
of the Auditor within the prescribed period; and
2. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to record entries in the Journals on a daily
basis or as soon as the financial documents are submitted by the Municipal
Treasurer’s Office to prevent delay in the preparation of the financial
statements.

Recommendations:
We recommend that the Management:
a. Shall conduct a regular monitoring of all on-going projects, programs
and activities and prepare monthly accomplishment reports.
b. Require the Municipal Accountant to submit Quarterly, Semestral and
Annual Reports to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed
period set by the Commission.

Recommendations:
We reiterate our recommendation that the Management:
a. Review the current accounting and reporting system of the LGU starting from
the determination of an accounting transaction, recording of the transactions,
posting the transactions to the journals, registries, subsidiary and general
journals, preparation of trial balance to the preparation of financial statements
to check and test whether it is in accordance with the established accounting
standards, rules and regulations. Immediately correct and rectify all errors and
inaccuracies observed.
b. Strictly maintain General Ledgers (GLs) for EACH (emphasis given) Balance
Sheet and Income Statement Account, as well as Subsidiary Ledgers (SLs) to
those accounts/category/accounting responsibility. Ensure also that the account
titles/names used and the amount which appears in the GL are the same with
that of the SL and the Financial Statements.
c. Prepare General Ledger for each account presented in the Financial Statements.
d. Maintain all the necessary journals, registries, ledgers and other relevant
supporting documents/ reports required by established accounting standards

(1) Require the Municipal Treasurer to submit the Report of Checks Issued and Report of Collections and Deposits daily to the Accounting Office pursuant to Section 55 on the Manual of NGAS and to facilitate submission to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period; (2) Instruct the Municipal Accountant to record entries in the Journals on a daily basis or as soon as the financial documents are submitted by the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to prevent delay in the preparation of the financial statements; and (3) Require the Municipal Accountant to submit financial reports, disbursement vouchers and supporting documents within the prescribed period.

Require the Municipal Accountant to submit Monthly, Quarterly, Semestral and Annual Reports to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period set by the Commission.

Monthly:
1. Report on Disaster Risk and Reduction Management Utilization of Funds – COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012

Quarterly:
1. Report on Programs, Projects and Activities (PPAs) – COA Circular 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013
2. Gender and Development Utilization – COA Circular 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014

Semestral Reports:
1. Unliquidated Cash Advances – COA Memo No. 2017-010 dated May 15, 2017
2. Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses – Office of the Auditor

Annually:
1. Gender and Development Accomplishment Report - COA Circular 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014
2. Report on Disaster Risk and Reduction Management Utilization of Funds – COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012

We recommended that the head of agency shall instruct the Accountant to prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statement (BRS on a monthly basis in accordance with COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996. The monthly BRS from January-November, 2012 shall be submitted immediately to the auditor for review and verification.

We further recommended that the management shall re-evaluate their internal control on accounting and financial operations to prevent possible loss of government funds due to not PPSAS compliance.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the head of agency shall instruct the Accountant to prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statement (BRS) on a monthly basis in accordance with COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996 for auditor’s immediate review and verification and to facilitate the immediate preparation of the necessary correction/adjusting entries for any discrepancies or errors or other reconciling items requiring corrections in order to reflect the correct balance of cash accounts in the financial statements. Also, bank reconciliation statements must be made for all bank accounts of the Municipality.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Management furnish the Audit Team with the copies of Memorandum of Agreement (MOA)/Trust Agreement on all funds transferred by the National Government Agencies (NGAs) as provided for in item 3.2.1 of COA Circular No. 2012 to evaluate whether the funds were utilized within the specified purpose.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive supervise and see to it that concerned officials prepare and submit these financial statements together with other relevant documents regularly pursuant to the stated regulations to enable the Auditor to verify, validate and audit the accounts thoroughly and promptly. Further, non-submission of these reports shall automatically cause the suspension of payment of their salaries until they shall have complied with the requirements of this Commission as provided in Section 122 paragraph 2 of PD 1445.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Management:
Require the Municipal Treasurer to submit the Report of Checks Issued and Report of Collections and Deposits daily to the Accounting Office pursuant to Section 55 on the Manual of NGAS and to facilitate submission to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period;
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to record entries in the Journals on a daily basis or as soon as the financial documents are submitted by the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to prevent delay in the preparation of the financial statements; and
Require the Municipal Accountant to submit financial reports, disbursement vouchers and supporting documents within the prescribed period.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor requires all Revenue Collectors prepare and submit the RAAF to the Municipal Treasurer so the latter can prepare the CRAAF and submit the monthly reports of accountability for accountable forms in the prescribed form provided in the Manual for NGAS for LGUs and submit the same not later than 5th day of the ensuing month to the COA Audit Team.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Management direct the personnel assigned to prepare the quarterly PPA reports to include non-infrastructure projects in compliance with COA Circular 2013-004.

Observe properly COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009 that prescribed the deadline for the submission of the receipt and disbursement records with all paid vouchers, official receipts, and reports and supporting documents within the first ten (10) days of the ensuing month.

Direct the Acting Municipal Accountant to present reliable data on the nature, status and balances of the accounts.

Refer to the available records and files of financial transactions of the agency in order to arrive at a reliable SL balances which correspond to the amount stated in the General Ledger of the major accounts.

   that prescribed the deadline for  the  submission  of  the  receipt  and
   disbursement records with all  paid  vouchers,  official  receipts,  and
   reports and supporting documents within the first ten (10) days  of  the
   ensuing month.

Direct the Acting Municipal Accountant to present reliable data on  the
   nature, status and balances of the accounts.

    the agency  in  order  to  arrive  at  a  reliable  SL  balances  which
    correspond to the amount stated in the  General  Ledger  of  the  major
    accounts.

We recommended that the head of agency shall instruct the Accountant  to
   prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statement (BRS on  a  monthly  basis  in
   accordance with COA Circular No.  96-011  dated  October  2,  1996.  The
   monthly BRS from October – December, 2016 shall be submitted immediately
   to the auditor for review and verification.


   We further recommended  that  the  management  shall  re-evaluate  their
   internal control on  accounting  and  financial  operations  to  prevent
   possible loss of government funds due to not PPSAS compliance.
We, therefore recommend that the agency’s officials and employees shall strictly adhere the pertinent section under P.D 1445 and COA Circular 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

We recommend that the head of agency shall instruct the Accountant to prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statement (BRS) on a monthly basis in accordance with COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996. The monthly BRS from-December, 2015 shall be submitted immediately to the auditor for review and verification.

Direct the Acting Municipal Accountant to present reliable data on the nature, status and balances of the accounts.

Refer to the available records and files of financial transactions of the agency in order to arrive at a reliable SL balances which correspond to the amount stated in the General Ledger of the major accounts.
